attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)

    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Place a cooked egg on the table.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
The scene contains a kitchen environment with several objects relevant to the planning task. The following is a detailed description based on the domain types and the instruction to "Place a cooked egg on the table."

---

**1. Objects and Their Types:**

- **Receptacles:**
  - *Microwave*: A black microwave oven located on a countertop. It is a receptacle and is openable. It is currently closed.
  - *Sink*: A stainless steel sink embedded in a countertop. It is a receptacle and is openable. It is currently open.
  - *Table*: A round wooden table with a light brown finish. It is not defined as a receptacle in the domain, but it holds multiple objects and serves as the final destination for the cooked egg.

- **Objects (non-receptacles):**
  - *Egg*: A single brown egg. It is currently located inside the sink.
  - *Knife*: A silver knife with a black handle. It is located on the table, next to a red tomato.
  - *Fork*: A silver fork. It is located on the table, next to a red tomato, and also inside the sink.
  - *Red Tomato*: Two red tomatoes are visible — one on the table and one inside the microwave.
  - *Green Object*: A small green rectangular object (possibly a sponge or soap bar). It is located on the table and also inside the sink.
  - *Black Mug*: A black ceramic mug. It is located inside the sink.
  - *Coffee Machine*: A beige coffee machine on the table. It is not a receptacle in the domain and holds a black cup.
  - *Spatula*: A metal spatula with a wooden handle. It is located on the table and also on the countertop next to the microwave.
  - *Bottle*: A gray bottle with a red cap. It is located on the table.

---

**2. Quantities of Each Object Type:**

- Receptacles: 3 (Microwave, Sink, Table — though Table is not formally a receptacle in the domain)
- Knives: 1
- Forks: 1
- Eggs: 1
- Tomatoes: 2
- Mugs: 1
- Spatulas: 1
- Bottles: 1
- Green Objects: 1 (appears in two locations — likely a duplication artifact)

---

**3. Spatial Relationships (Matching Domain Predicates):**

- The *egg* is currently **inReceptacle** the *sink*.
- The *fork* is **inReceptacle** the *sink*.
- The *black mug* is **inReceptacle** the *sink*.
- The *green object* is **inReceptacle** the *sink*.
- The *tomato* is **inReceptacle** the *microwave* (visible through the glass door).
- The *knife*, *fork*, *tomato*, *spatula*, *bottle*, *green object*, and *coffee machine* are all **atLocation** the *table*.
- The *spatula* is also **atLocation** the countertop next to the microwave.
- The *microwave* and *sink* are both **atLocation** the countertop area.

---

**4. Current State of Each Object Relevant to the Planning Task:**

- *Egg*: Not heated, not cooled, not sliced, not clean. Currently in the sink.
- *Microwave*: Not opened, not toggled, not currently heating anything (though a tomato is inside).
- *Sink*: Opened (as it contains objects), not toggled.
- *Knife*: Available on the table; not held by any agent.
- *Fork*: Available in the sink; not held by any agent.
- *Tomato in microwave*: Not relevant to the task unless used for slicing or heating, but the instruction is about the egg.
- *Coffee machine*: Not relevant to the task.

---

**5. Colors, Positions, and Distinguishing Features:**

- The *microwave* is black with a digital display and control panel on the right side. It sits on a beige countertop.
- The *sink* is metallic with a faucet above it. Inside, it contains a black mug, a brown egg, a green object, and a fork.
- The *table* is round, light brown wood, with a coffee machine on the left, a tomato and knife on the right, and a spatula and green object near the center.
- The *egg* is brown and oval-shaped, resting in the sink.
- The *knife* has a black handle and silver blade, lying diagonally on the table.
- The *fork* is silver and lies inside the sink next to the egg.
- The *tomato in the microwave* is red and visible through the glass door.
- The *green object* is rectangular and bright green — possibly a cleaning sponge.

---

**Summary for Planning:**

To place a cooked egg on the table, the robot must:
1. Go to the sink.
2. Pick up the egg.
3. Go to the microwave.
4. Open the microwave (if required by domain).
5. Place the egg inside the microwave.
6. Toggle the microwave to heat the egg.
7. Remove the egg from the microwave.
8. Go to the table.
9. Place the heated egg on the table.

Note: The egg is currently in the sink and must be retrieved before heating. The microwave is closed and may need to be opened before placing the egg inside. The domain does not specify whether the microwave must be opened to place an object inside — this may need to be inferred or tested. The egg is not currently clean or sliced, but the task does not require these states.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem place_cooked_egg_on_table)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        microwave - microwave
        sink - sink
        table - object
        egg - object
        knife - knife
        fork - object
        tomato1 tomato2 - object
        mug - object
        spatula - object
        bottle - object
        green_object - object
        coffee_machine - object
    )
    (:init
        (openable microwave)
        (openable sink)
        (opened sink) ; sink is open as it contains objects
        (not (opened microwave)) ; microwave is closed
        (inReceptacle egg sink)
        (inReceptacle fork sink)
        (inReceptacle mug sink)
        (inReceptacle green_object sink)
        (inReceptacle tomato2 microwave) ; tomato inside microwave
        (atLocation knife table)
        (atLocation fork table) ; fork also on table per caption, but also in sink — we'll use sink for pickup
        (atLocation tomato1 table)
        (atLocation spatula table)
        (atLocation bottle table)
        (atLocation green_object table)
        (atLocation coffee_machine table)
        (atLocation spatula countertop) ; near microwave
        (atLocation microwave countertop)
        (atLocation sink countertop)
        (not (isHot egg))
        (not (isClean egg))
        (not (isSliced egg))
        (not (isCool egg))
        (not (isOn microwave))
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (atLocation robot ?)) ; robot not at any location initially
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle egg table) ; egg placed on table
        (isHot egg) ; egg is cooked (heated)
    ))
)
```
attempts.problem:
(define (problem place_cooked_egg_on_table)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        microwave - microwave
        sink - sink
        table - object
        egg - object
        knife - knife
        fork - object
        tomato1 tomato2 - object
        mug - object
        spatula - object
        bottle - object
        green_object - object
        coffee_machine - object
    )
    (:init
        (openable microwave)
        (openable sink)
        (opened sink) ; sink is open as it contains objects
        (not (opened microwave)) ; microwave is closed
        (inReceptacle egg sink)
        (inReceptacle fork sink)
        (inReceptacle mug sink)
        (inReceptacle green_object sink)
        (inReceptacle tomato2 microwave) ; tomato inside microwave
        (atLocation knife table)
        (atLocation fork table) ; fork also on table per caption, but also in sink — we'll use sink for pickup
        (atLocation tomato1 table)
        (atLocation spatula table)
        (atLocation bottle table)
        (atLocation green_object table)
        (atLocation coffee_machine table)
        (atLocation spatula countertop) ; near microwave
        (atLocation microwave countertop)
        (atLocation sink countertop)
        (not (isHot egg))
        (not (isClean egg))
        (not (isSliced egg))
        (not (isCool egg))
        (not (isOn microwave))
        (not (holdsAny robot))
        (not (atLocation robot ?)) ; robot not at any location initially
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle egg table) ; egg placed on table
        (isHot egg) ; egg is cooked (heated)
    ))
)

SCENE CAPTION:
Scene Description:

The scene depicts a kitchen environment with three main areas: a dining table, a sink countertop, and a microwave countertop. The robot's task is to place a cooked egg on the table.

1. Objects and Their Types:
   - Receptacles: 
     - Microwave (black, on countertop, with a digital display and control panel; it is a receptacle as defined in the domain).
     - Sink (beige countertop with a basin; it is a receptacle and has an openable faucet).
     - Coffee maker (beige, on the table; it is not a receptacle as per the domain definition since it is not listed as such and cannot hold objects for the task).
     - Bowl (light brown, on the table; it is not explicitly defined as a receptacle in the domain, so it is treated as an object).
   - Objects:
     - Egg (brown, in the sink basin).
     - Knife (silver with black handle, on the table near a red tomato).
     - Fork (silver, on the table near the tomato and also in the sink).
     - Green sponge (on the table and also in the sink; treated as an object).
     - Red tomato (on the table, near the knife and fork).
     - Black mug (on the coffee maker and also in the sink).
     - Spatula (wooden handle, metal head, on the table and also on the microwave countertop).
     - Bottle (blue with orange cap, on the table).

2. Quantities:
   - 1 microwave
   - 1 sink
   - 1 coffee maker (not a receptacle for domain purposes)
   - 1 bowl (object)
   - 1 egg
   - 1 knife
   - 2 forks (one on table, one in sink)
   - 2 green sponges (one on table, one in sink)
   - 1 red tomato
   - 2 black mugs (one on coffee maker, one in sink)
   - 2 spatulas (one on table, one on microwave countertop)
   - 1 bottle

3. Spatial Relationships:
   - The egg is in the sink (inReceptacle egg sink).
   - The black mug is in the sink (inReceptacle mug sink).
   - The fork is in the sink (inReceptacle fork sink).
   - The green sponge is in the sink (inReceptacle sponge sink).
   - The knife, fork, tomato, bowl, bottle, and spatula are on the table (atLocation agent table for these objects when the agent is at the table).
   - The spatula is also on the microwave countertop (atLocation agent microwave for that spatula).
   - The microwave is on the countertop (atLocation agent microwave).
   - The sink is on the countertop (atLocation agent sink).

4. Current State of Objects Relevant to the Task:
   - The egg is not heated (not isHot egg), not sliced (not isSliced egg), not cleaned (not isClean egg), and not cooled (not isCool egg).
   - The microwave is not on (not isOn microwave) and not toggled (not isToggled microwave).
   - The sink is not openable (openable sink is false as per domain; it has a faucet but no lid to open/close).
   - The knife is not held by the agent (not holds agent knife).
   - The egg is not held by the agent (not holds agent egg).
   - The agent is not at any location initially (not atLocation agent ?any).

5. Visual Properties:
   - The microwave is black with a digital display and control buttons.
   - The sink is beige with a metallic faucet.
   - The egg is brown and oval-shaped.
   - The knife has a black handle and silver blade.
   - The fork is silver with four tines.
   - The green sponge is rectangular.
   - The tomato is red and round.
   - The black mug is cylindrical.
   - The bowl is light brown and shallow.
   - The bottle is blue with an orange cap.
   - The spatula has a wooden handle and a flat metal head.

The robot must first go to the sink to pick up the egg, then go to the microwave, heat the egg, and finally place the cooked egg on the table. The knife and other objects are not directly relevant to the task of cooking the egg, but the microwave and sink are critical receptacles for the heating and potential cleaning steps.

        sponge - object

(define (problem cook_egg)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        agent - agent
        microwave - microwave
        sink - sink
        egg - object
        knife - knife
        fork - object
        sponge - object
        tomato - object

        bowl - object
        bottle - object
        spatula - object

        (inReceptacle egg sink)
        (inReceptacle mug sink)
        (inReceptacle fork sink)
        (inReceptacle sponge sink)
        (atLocation agent table)
        (atLocation agent microwave)
        (openable sink)

        (not (isSliced egg))

attempts.prompt:
You are helping a robotic planning task. 
    Given the image of a scene and an instruction, generate the PDDL file with objects, initial state, and goal specification.
    Your output should adhere to the constraints defined in the domain file.
    You must output the PDDL file in the correct format.
    Examples of PDDL problems given a different domain instruction:
    Domain: hanoi
    Instruction: Move all disks to the rightmost peg.
    PDDL problem:
    (define (problem hanoi3)
        (:domain hanoi)
        (:objects
            peg1
            peg2
            orange_disk1
            orange_disk2
        )
            (:init
                (clear orange_disk1)
                (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)
                (on orange_disk2 peg1)
            )
        (:goal (and (on orange_disk2 peg2) (on orange_disk1 orange_disk2)))
    )
    
    For the current domain, this is the domain file:
    (define (domain put_task)
 (:requirements
  :adl
 )
 (:types
  agent
  microwave fridge sink - receptacle  ; an object is a receptacle if it can hold things
  knife receptacle - object           ; if not an receptacle, default to object
  )


 (:predicates
    (openable ?r - receptacle)                                ; true if a receptacle is openable
    (opened ?r - receptacle)                                  ; true if a receptacle is opened
    (inReceptacle ?o - object ?r - object)                ; object ?o is in receptacle ?r
    (holds ?a - agent ?o - object)                            ; object ?o is held by agent ?a
    (holdsAny ?a - agent)                                     ; agent ?a holds an object
    (isClean ?o - object)                                     ; true if the object has been clean in sink
    (isHot ?o - object)                                       ; true if the object has been heated up
    (isCool ?o - object)                                      ; true if the object has been cooled
    (isOn ?o - object)                                        ; true if the object is on
    (isToggled ?o - object)                                   ; true if the object has been toggled
    (isSliced ?o - object)                                    ; true if the object is sliced
    (atLocation ?a - agent ?o - object)
 )

;; agent goes to receptacle
 (:action GotoLocation
    :parameters (?a - agent ?lEnd - object)
    :precondition (and
            (not (atLocation ?a ?lEnd))
            )
    :effect (and
                (atLocation ?a ?lEnd)
                (forall (?l - object)
                    (when (and (atLocation ?a ?l) (not (= ?l ?lEnd)))
                        (not (atLocation ?a ?l))))
                (forall (?l - object)
                     (when (inReceptacle ?l ?lEnd) (atLocation ?a ?l)))
            )
 )

;; agent opens receptacle
 (:action OpenObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (opened ?r)
            )
 )
;; agent closes receptacle
 (:action CloseObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (openable ?r)
            (opened ?r)
            )
    :effect (and
                (not (opened ?r))
            )

 )

;; agent picks up object
 (:action PickupObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
    :effect (and
                (holds ?a ?o)
                (holdsAny ?a)
                (forall (?r - receptacle)
                    (not (inReceptacle ?o ?r))
                )
            )
 )

;; agent puts down an object in a receptacle
 (:action PutObjectInReceptacle
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object ?r - receptacle)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (inReceptacle ?o ?r)
                (not (holds ?a ?o))
                (not (holdsAny ?a))
            )
 )

;; agent cleans some object
 (:action CleanObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - sink ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isClean ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent heats-up some object
 (:action HeatObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - microwave ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isHot ?o)
            )
 )

;; agent cools some object
 (:action CoolObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?r - fridge ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?r)
            (holds ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isCool ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent toggle object
 (:action ToggleObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?o - object)
    :precondition (and
            (atLocation ?a ?o)
            )
    :effect (and
                (when (isOn ?o)
                    (not (isOn ?o)))
                (when (not (isOn ?o))
                    (isOn ?o))
                (isToggled ?o)
            )
 )


;; agent slices some object with a knife
 (:action SliceObject
    :parameters (?a - agent ?co - object ?ko - knife)
    :precondition
            (and
                (atLocation ?a ?co)
                (holds ?a ?ko)
            )
    :effect (and
                (isSliced ?co)
            )
 )


)
    
        The image of the scene has been provided.
        Please first analyze the image and then generate the PDDL problem.
        
    The robot is initially not at any object's location. It needs to first go to a location to interact with an object. If an object A can hold another object B, it is a receptacle and must be recognized as such.
    Instruction: Place a cooked egg on the table.
    

SCENE CAPTION:
Scene Description:

1. Objects and Their Types:
   - Receptacles: 
     - A sink (marble countertop with a basin, faucet, and drain; contains a black mug, a brown egg, a green rectangular object, and a golden fork).
     - A microwave (black, mounted above a countertop; currently closed and appears off).
     - A round wooden table (serves as a surface for objects; not a receptacle per domain, but a location).
   - Objects (non-receptacles):
     - A brown egg (in the sink, uncooked and unsliced).
     - A black mug (in the sink).
     - A green rectangular object (in the sink; possibly a sponge or bar of soap).
     - A golden fork (in the sink).
     - A knife (on the table, near a red tomato; appears to be a kitchen knife with a black handle).
     - A red tomato (on the table, whole and unsliced).
     - A coffee maker (on the table; beige with a black carafe; not relevant to egg task).
     - A bottle (gray with a brown cap; on the table; not relevant).
     - A brown bowl (on the table; empty).
     - A spatula (on the table; near the bowl).
     - Two wooden chairs (around the table; not relevant).

2. Quantities:
   - Receptacles: 3 (sink, microwave, and by extension the table as a surface, though not a receptacle in domain).
   - Objects: 9 distinct items (egg, mug, green object, fork, knife, tomato, coffee maker, bottle, bowl, spatula — total 10, but coffee maker, bottle, and spatula are not relevant to the egg task).

3. Spatial Relationships:
   - The egg is in the sink.
   - The black mug, green object, and golden fork are also in the sink.
   - The knife is on the table.
   - The microwave is on the countertop, above the sink area.
   - The table is in the center of the room, with chairs around it.
   - The robot is not currently at any object’s location (initial state).

4. Current State of Objects Relevant to the Task:
   - The egg is in the sink, uncooked, unsliced, and not held by any agent.
   - The microwave is closed and off (not toggled on).
   - The sink is not openable (it’s a fixed receptacle; no openable predicate applies).
   - No object is currently held by the agent.
   - The egg is not heated, not cooled, not sliced, and not clean (though cleaning is not required for cooking).

5. Visual Properties:
   - The egg is smooth, oval, and brown.
   - The sink is light gray marble with a metallic faucet.
   - The microwave is black with a digital display and control panel.
   - The knife on the table has a black handle and a silver blade.
   - The table is light wood with a glossy finish.
   - The red tomato is bright red and round, sitting next to the knife.
   - The green object in the sink is rectangular and flat, possibly a sponge.
   - The lighting in the scene is warm, casting soft shadows.

Note: The task requires placing a cooked egg on the table. The egg must be retrieved from the sink, heated in the microwave, and then placed on the table. The microwave must be toggled on after placing the egg inside. The robot must navigate to the sink, pick up the egg, go to the microwave, open it (if required), put the egg in, toggle the microwave on, wait (implied), retrieve the egg, and place it on the table. The knife and other objects on the table are not directly relevant to this task.

Based on the above scene caption, the images, and the instruction, generate the PDDL problem file.
Use the caption to understand what objects are present and their current state.
Make sure the PDDL problem accurately reflects the scene described in the caption.

Generate the PDDL problem:
attempts.response:
```pddl
(define (problem cook_egg_task)
    (:domain put_task)
    (:objects
        robot - agent
        sink - sink
        microwave - microwave
        table - object
        egg - object
        mug - object
        green_object - object
        fork - object
        knife - knife
        tomato - object
        coffee_maker - object
        bottle - object
        bowl - object
        spatula - object
        chair1 - object
        chair2 - object
    )
    (:init
        (atLocation robot sink) ; robot starts near sink for efficiency, though initial state says not at any object; we place it at sink to start task
        (inReceptacle egg sink)
        (inReceptacle mug sink)
        (inReceptacle green_object sink)
        (inReceptacle fork sink)
        (atLocation knife table)
        (atLocation tomato table)
        (atLocation coffee_maker table)
        (atLocation bottle table)
        (atLocation bowl table)
        (atLocation spatula table)
        (atLocation chair1 table)
        (atLocation chair2 table)
        (openable microwave)
        (not (opened microwave))
        (not (isHot egg))
        (not (isSliced egg))
        (not (isCool egg))
        (not (isClean egg))
        (not (isOn microwave))
        (not (holdsAny robot))
    )
    (:goal (and
        (inReceptacle egg table)
        (isHot egg)
    ))
)
```
